data.title: RAK Properties' FY24 Net Profit, Revenue Climb
data.description:
RAK Properties (ADX:RAKPROP) said Wednesday that its net profit and revenue for full-year 2024 jumped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 280.9 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 201.8 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.111 dirham from 0.097 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed property developer's revenue was 1.41 billion dirhams, compared with 1 billion dirhams earlier.

data.title: Hawkish BOJ Board Member Calls for Potentially Faster Rate Hikes — Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

A hawkish member of the Bank of Japan's policy board on Thursday called for potentially faster interest-rate increases, sending the yen to its strongest level against the dollar in eight weeks.

In a speech to business leaders in Nagano prefecture in central Japan, Naoki Tamura said that the central bank should raise rates to 1% or higher in the fiscal half starting in October. That level is likely consistent with a neutral rate setting that is neither restrictive nor stimulating for the economy, he added.

"Raising short-term interest rates to at least around 1% in the latter half of fiscal 2025 is necessary to reduce upside risk to prices and achieve the price stability target in a sustainable and stable manner," he said.

Tamura's comments suggest that the central bank may tighten monetary policy more quickly than some economists and investors have been expecting. Consensus views are for one or two more 25-basis-point hikes in the next fiscal year, but the board member suggested that the policy rate could go beyond 1%.

After his speech, the yen went to around 151.80 against the dollar, the strongest level since Dec. 11.

Many economists expect the BOJ to raise the policy rate again later this year, but some have cautioned that the next move requires careful consideration as rates approach levels Japan hasn't experienced in 30 years.

Tamura is a former executive at Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group and considered hawkish. He proposed a rate increase in December, citing increased risks of higher consumer prices. The proposal was defeated by a majority vote, but the policy board then decided to lift interest rates to 0.5% from 0.25% at the bank's January meeting.

Tamura's comments could well be testing the waters, seeing how markets react to the idea of rate hikes at least every six months, said Mizuho Securities economist Yusuke Matsuo.

The central bank has been making efforts to improve its communication to markets, with the goal of minimizing disruptive reactions to its policy moves.

That said, Matsuo thinks the BOJ may have to stop once rates reach 0.75% because he expects inflation to slow.

Tamura seems to have a different outlook, saying in the speech that the bank should be able to achieve its goal of creating sustainable, stable 2% inflation in the second half of fiscal 2025 when wage trends among small companies become clearer.

Growth and mild inflation backed by higher wages is a central goal for the BOJ.

Tamura also voiced concern that inflation--which has been above 2% for nearly three years--and a recent spike in rice prices may hurt consumer sentiment. Consumer inflation picked up to 3.6% on year in December, compared with 2.9% in November.

Even if the BOJ raises interest rates to 0.75%, monetary conditions will remain easy as real interest rates will stay negative, the board member said.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Paycor HCM Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Paycor HCM Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 14 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 11 cents. The mean expectation of seventeen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 12 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 11 cents to 13 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 13.1% to $180.44 million from a year ago; analysts expected $177.16 million.

  • Paycor HCM Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 1 cent​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $2.05 million.

  • Paycor HCM Inc shares had risen by 19.2% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 13 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the software peer group is also "hold"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Paycor HCM Inc is $22.50

data.title: India's Sula Vineyards falls on lower Q3 profit
data.description:
** Shares of Indian winemaker Sula Vineyards down ~4% at 350.3 rupees

** Co reports a 35% fall in Q3 profit on lower demand amid a slowdown in urban consumption and election-related disruptions in Maharashtra state, a key market for co

** Total revenue down 0.4% in Q3, while total expenses up 11.4%

** Sula gets about 90% of its revenue from urban markets

** Sula's profit margin contracts to 24.8% from 33.5% last year

** Including session's decline, shares have fallen 45.5% y/y

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on Feb 6
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira traded at 35.9390 against the U.S. dollar at 0452 GMT, after closing at 35.8925 on Wednesday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.89% lower at 9,719.81 points on Wednesday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asia shares rose on Thursday, tracking gains on Wall Street following a see-saw session, while U.S. Treasury yields came under pressure after mixed economic data.

EARTHQUAKE ANNIVERSARY

Turkey marks the two-year anniversary of its worst natural disaster: the 7.8-magnitude earthquake that killed some 53,000 people in the country and another 6,000 in neighbouring Syria on a winter night in 2023.

President Tayyip Erdogan, main opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel, and several ministers, including those responsible for labor, culture, energy, trade, agriculture, and industry, will attend ceremonies across the earthquake region.

ISTANBUL MAYOR

Turkish prosecutors have issued a third indictment against Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu - a potential challenger to President Tayyip Erdogan - for remarks criticising the city's prosecutor, according to the indictment and local media.

ISLAMIC STATE

Turkey, Iraq, Syria and Jordan will take steps toward jointly combating Islamic State in the region and they aim to hold a first meeting on the issue in Jordan, Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said on Wednesday.

PARLIAMENT

Turkish Parliament convenes to discuss items on its agenda (1100).

POPULATION STATISTICS

Turkish Statistical Institute announces 2024 address-based population registration system results (0700).

BANKING DATA

Turkish Central Bank releases weekly money and banking data, foreign exchange reserves, and securities movements (1130).

data.title: Whitbread Prices Offer of GBP400 Million Guaranteed Notes
data.description:
Whitbread priced an offering of 400 million pounds sterling of 5.50% guaranteed notes due 2032, according to a Wednesday filing.

The notes are expected to be issued on Feb. 12. The British hotel and restaurant company earmarked proceeds from the offering for general corporate expenditures, including debt refinancing.

data.title: Market Chatter: Singapore's Real Estate Transaction Volumes Rebound in 2024 with 28% Increase
data.description:
Singapore's real estate transaction volumes increased by 28% to SG$28.6 billion in 2024 following a 30.3% decline in 2023, according to a report by Singapore Business Review on Wednesday.

The increase in transaction volume was mostly due to a surge in transactions within the industrial and logistic sectors, the report added.

According to CBRE's 2025 Singapore Real Estate Market Outlook report, retail prime rents are expected to increase by 2% to 3% in 2025, led by Orchard Road and City Hall/Marina Centre.

Residential prices are expected to rise by 3% to 6% with developers to launch about 14,000 units during the year, the report added.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Lindian Resources Gives $1.3 Million Site Access Road Contract for Malawian Rare Earths Project; Shares Down 5%
data.description:
Lindian Resources gave a $1.3 million site access road contract to European construction group Mota-Engil for its Kangankunde rare earths project in Malawi, the company said in a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The contract includes the intersection of the access road with the main M1 highway, 5 kilometers of access road works, and civil works around the mining lease access area with an associated security area, added the filing.

Company shares dropped almost 5% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Shanghai Junshi Biosciences Gets Clinical Trial Approval for Anti-tumor Drug; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Shanghai Junshi Biosciences obtained approval from China's drug administrator to conduct clinical trials for its AWT020 sterile powder for injection.

AWT020 will be carried out as a single drug and in combination with other treatments in patients with advanced malignant tumors, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the pharmaceutical company rose 3% in recent trade on the Shanghai bourse.

Qisda's Consolidated Sales Rise Marginally in January
data.description:
Qisda Corp.'s consolidated sales for January edged up 1.63% year over year to NT$16.5 billion from NT$16.3 billion, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Shares of the technology group gained about 2% recently.

data.title: Keppel's Asset Monetization Gathers Pace — Market Talk
data.description: Keppel seems to be on track to meet its target of S$10 billion-S$12 billion in asset monetization by 2026, supported by opportunities such as telco M1 and rigs, CGS International analysts Lim Siew Khee and Kenneth Tan say in a note. Keppel's cumulative asset monetization since October 2020 is near S$7 billion, including S$1.5 billion in 2024, the analysts note. The company's 2024 net profit was in line with CGS and consensus forecasts. The brokerage raises its target to S$9.28 from S$8.78 after rolling forward its valuation. CGS maintains an add rating as it reckons Keppel is transitioning into a streamlined, asset-light global asset manager. The growth potential of Keppel's new business engines, including its data-center portfolio, also looks attractive to CGS. Shares are up 1.8% at S$6.80. (hoishan.chan@wsj.com)

data.title: India Central Bank Tipped to Begin Easing Cycle, WSJ Poll Shows — Market Talk
data.description: India's Central Bank Is Tipped to Cut Its Policy Repo Rate by 25 Bps to 6.25% on Friday, According to All 12 Economists Polled by The Wall Street Journal. The government's focus on narrowing the fiscal deficit, and high frequency indicators pointing to a growth slowdown are key factors supporting expectations of a rate cut, DBS Group Research's Radhika Rao says in a note. With inflation falling, room for rate cuts and easier liquidity has opened up, HSBC economists say. Citi Research analysts expect a 25bp cut, highlighting that this has been the longest period the RBI has been on hold after the end of a rate hike cycle. However, Citi notes some investors still expect the RBI to stay on hold due to global uncertainties. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Mitsubishi Chemical 3Q Basic Materials, Polymers Core Op Loss Y0.8B Vs Profit Y0.8B
A.P. Møller - Mærsk to Initiate DKK14 Billion Share Buyback Program
data.description:
A.P. Møller - Mærsk (MAERSK-A.CO, MAERSK-B.CO) said Wednesday it plans to commence a 12-month share buyback program totaling 14.4 billion Danish kroner.

The first phase will commence on Friday and run until Aug. 6 with 7.2 billion kroner worth of shares to be repurchased. Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken was appointed as lead manager for the initial phase.

The shipping giant said the program is intended to adjust its capital structure with the repurchased shares to be ultimately canceled.

data.title: Tecom Group Logs Higher FY24 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Tecom Group (DFM:TECOM) said Thursday that its profit and revenue for the full-year 2024 climbed year over year.

Profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 1.23 billion Emirati dirhams, compared with 1.08 billion dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.25 dirham from 0.22 dirham.

The Dubai-listed property developer's revenue was 2.40 billion dirhams, compared with 2.17 billion dirhams previously.

Meanwhile, the board proposed an unchanged cash dividend of 0.08 dirham per share for the period, subject to approval from shareholders at an upcoming annual general meeting on March 10.

The board also reviewed the interim cash dividend for the second half of 2025, which is expected to increase by 10%.

data.title: Guosen Securities' Asset Management Arm Secures Securities, Futures Business License
data.description:
Guosen Securities' subsidiary, Guosen Securities Asset Management, obtained a license to operate securities and futures businesses, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

The scope of the securities and futures business is securities asset management. The asset management unit will also be allowed to operate as a securities brokerage, securities investment consultant and financial consultant for securities trading and other similar functions, the Chinese brokerage said.

Guosen Securities' shares rose more than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: Singapore to Contribute $87 Million to IDA
data.description:
Singapore has pledged to inject $87 million in International Development Association across three years, according to a Singapore Business Review report on Wednesday.

According to second minister of finance, Indranee Rajah, the amount represents a 24% increase from the country's previous contribution.

IDA is a World Bank project that focuses on providing grants and low-interest loans to low-income countries, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: India's benchmarks pause ahead of key earnings, RBI rate decision
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes were little changed on Thursday, as investors awaited key corporate earnings and the Reserve Bank of India's rate decision.

The Nifty 50 was off 0.14% at 23,664.9 points as of 9:47 a.m. IST, while the BSE Sensex had slipped 0.14% to 78,158.1.

They had opened about 0.3% higher after logging marginal losses in the previous session, with sentiment remaining neutral ahead of the RBI's widely expected first interest rate cut in nearly five years.

A rate cut, if it happens, will follow less than a week after the government, in the federal budget, cut personal tax rates to stimulate consumption.

The benchmarks have taken "a pause after the rebound" on Tuesday and are awaiting the RBI's rate decision and commentary and some key Nifty 50 companies for directional triggers, said Prashanth Tapse, senior vice president of research at Mehta Equities.

Six Nifty companies — State Bank of India , Trent , Bharti Airtel , Hero MotoCorp , Britannia and ITC — are due to report their quarterly earnings on the day.

Eight of the 13 major sectors declined in morning trade.

The broader, more domestically focused small-caps and mid-caps traded flat, erasing opening gains.

Among individual stocks, food delivery and quick commerce platform Swiggy lost about 8% to a record low after reporting a wider loss in the third quarter.

Winemaker Sula Vineyards dropped 4% after posting a 35% slide in its December-quarter profit, hurt by a slowdown in urban consumption.

Trucking firm VRL Logistics jumped 14% after logging a rise in quarterly profit.
